skills.name: 10-factor-html
skills.title: 10-factor-html
skills.summary: Improve HTML, landing pages, docs pages, static previews, and generated web pages for semantic structure, responsiveness, accessibility, SEO, performance, visual polish, and Agent handoff quality.
recommended_trigger_phrases: Use the 10-factor-html skill to improve this HTML page for semantic structure, responsiveness, accessibility, SEO, performance, and visual polish.

Inspect relevant files and infer missing context conservatively.

skills.name: 11-factor-cli
skills.title: 11-factor-cli
skills.summary: Audit and refactor command-line products so they are installable, understandable, scriptable, observable, testable, and release-ready.
recommended_trigger_phrases: Use the 11-factor-cli skill to audit this CLI for installability, command UX, configuration, error handling, logging, testing, packaging, and release readiness.
expected_inputs: package metadata such as package.json, pyproject.toml, Cargo.toml, go.mod, or setup config
Example commands, tests, workflows, and release scripts when available
expected_outputs: CLI audit report with evidence and score
Refactor plan ranked by user impact and release readiness
Command UX improvements with example commands
Open issues, assumptions, and next iteration suggestions
review_protocol: Identify the CLI runtime, entrypoint, installation path, and core commands.
Run or statically inspect help output, examples, config handling, and error messages.
Score the CLI against the 11-factor checklist.
Prioritize fixes that make the CLI installable, testable, predictable, and scriptable.
Return an audit report and concrete refactor plan.

skills.name: 13-factor-product-readiness
skills.title: 13-factor-product-readiness
skills.summary: Audit and refactor repositories for product positioning, onboarding, examples, validation, release readiness, GitHub polish, Agent-native usage, and launch quality.
recommended_trigger_phrases: Use the 13-factor-product-readiness skill to audit this repository for launch readiness, onboarding, product polish, release discipline, and open-source credibility.
Use the 13-factor-product-readiness skill to turn this demo repository into a publishable product-quality open-source project.
review_protocol: Identify target user, product promise, and primary use case.
Review first 30 seconds and first 5 minutes of onboarding.
Apply the 13-factor checklist and score the repository.
Prioritize launch blockers before polish.
Recommend paired technical Factor Skills for follow-up.

skills.name: 14-factor-security-privacy
skills.title: 14-factor-security-privacy
skills.summary: Audit repositories for secrets, permissions, data handling, dependency hygiene, CI/CD safety, Agent/tool boundaries, privacy posture, and release blockers.
recommended_trigger_phrases: Use the 14-factor-security-privacy skill to audit this repository for secrets, permissions, data handling, dependency hygiene, CI/CD safety, and release blockers.
Use the 14-factor-security-privacy skill after 12-factor-agents to review tool permissions, prompt injection risk, destructive actions, secrets, and log safety.
Use the 14-factor-security-privacy skill with 8-factor-github-actions to check workflow permissions, secrets exposure, release artifact safety, and deployment approval gates.
review_protocol: Identify data, credentials, tools, integrations, workflows, and release context.
Map trust boundaries and high-risk operations.
Review secret hygiene, configuration, permissions, logs, dependencies, and CI/CD.
Apply the 14-factor checklist and scoring rubric.
Prioritize critical blockers before polish.
Record validation evidence and limits honestly.

skills.name: 15-factor-observability
skills.title: 15-factor-observability
skills.summary: Audit runtime visibility, structured logs, metrics, traces, validation artifacts, failure diagnostics, release evidence, privacy-safe debugging, and Agent handoff readiness.
recommended_trigger_phrases: Use the 15-factor-observability skill to audit this Agent project for logs, traces, tool-call evidence, validation artifacts, and debuggability.
Use the 15-factor-observability skill with 14-factor-security-privacy to make this app easier to debug without leaking secrets or user data.
Use the 15-factor-observability skill with 8-factor-github-actions to improve workflow summaries, failure logs, release artifacts, and deployment diagnostics.
expected_inputs: README, quickstart, docs, examples, and troubleshooting notes
source files, run entrypoints, CLI commands, workflows, and tests
logs, traces, validation outputs, audit artifacts, and failure reports
Agent traces, tool-call records, RAG retrieval logs, or MCP invocation records when available
expected_outputs: validation plan and paired Skill recommendation
review_protocol: Map the system's key run paths, dependencies, and failure points.
Review logs, errors, metrics, traces, artifacts, issue templates, and release evidence.
Check whether debugging data is useful while remaining privacy-safe.
Apply the 15-factor checklist and scoring rubric.
Produce an observability audit report and prioritized refactor plan.
Recommend paired Skills when observability issues overlap with security, Agent, RAG, MCP, GitHub Actions, CLI, docs, or product readiness.

skills.name: 5-factor-evals
skills.title: 5-factor-evals
skills.summary: Audit evaluation, regression testing, benchmark, golden-case, failure taxonomy, and release gate systems for Agent-native product projects.
recommended_trigger_phrases: Use the 5-factor-evals skill to audit this Agent project for golden cases, regression tests, failure taxonomy, and release gates.
Use the 5-factor-evals skill to add a repeatable evaluation plan before we refactor this RAG pipeline.
Use the 5-factor-evals skill as a secondary review after 12-factor-agents and 9-factor-rag.
review_protocol: Identify the product behavior that must stay reliable.
Review tests, examples, fixtures, logs, CI, and release notes.
Apply the 5-factor checklist and scoring rubric.
Recommend a minimum useful evaluation harness before larger refactors.
Record validation results and validation limits honestly.

skills.name: 6-factor-docs

known onboarding friction or user questions
review_protocol: Identify the target reader and first useful action.
Review README, docs tree, examples, and release notes for consistency.
Apply the 6-factor checklist and score the documentation.
Prioritize adoption blockers before polish.
Record validation limits honestly.
